calendar.matters:
Notice Of Petitioners Motion For Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-20-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER RUSSELL QUINAN's Motion For Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus

Petitioner Russell Quinan's motion for writ of mandamus is DENIED. California Corporations Code section 1600(a)(1) allows inspection of corporate books and records "upon the written demand on the corporation of any shareholder." Section 185 defines "shareholder" as "one who is a holder of record of shares." (Corp. Code Section 185.) On July 10, 2020, Respondent Traction Corporation's shareholders unanimously approved a reverse stock split, which liquidated Petitioner's interest in Traction Corporation. This vote satisfied the notice requirements of Corporations Code section 601 and was certified by the California Secretary of State.

Petitioner contends that although he did not make his shareholder demand until October 2020, after the vote, the July 10 shareholder vote was void, and that he therefore remains a shareholder with rights to inspection. The undated shareholder list attached to the Restis Declaration (Ex. A) does not establish that Petitioner was a shareholder when he made that demand. Petitioner presents no authority to support his argument that a former shareholder who challenges the validity of a shareholder vote retains rights under section 1600, nor has he obtained any relief setting aside that vote. Petitioner therefore fails to persuade this Court that he is entitled to rights that, by the plain statutory language, may be invoked only by shareholders of record. (See Hagan v. Fairfield (1960) 183 Cal.App.2d 704, 706 [petition that failed to allege that petitioner was a shareholder of record did not allege a proper basis for issuing a writ of mandamus]; Acosta v. Pacific Enterprises (9th Cir. 1991) 950 F.2d 611, fn. 4 ["Because Acosta is not a shareholder of record, he could not bring such an action [under section 1600(c)]; under California corporations law, only shareholders of record are entitled to inspect and copy a list of shareholders."]; see also Wolf v. CDS Devco (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 903, 908, 916-919 [former corporate director lacks standing to invoke director's absolute right under section 1602 to inspect corporate records, despite claim that he was unlawfully removed from the board].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WILLIAM R. RESTIS, Esq. of the Restis Law Firm, P.C. (Tel: 619-270-8383) for Petitioner Russell Quinan;
Atty. SEAN E. MAKARIN, Esq. (Tel: 415-777-1800) for Respondent Traction Corporation.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT TRACTION CORPORATION's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Restricting Discovery
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
William Restis, Esq. (619-270-8383; william@restislaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Sean Makarin, Esq. (sean@makarinlaw.com) for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT TRACTION CORPORATION's Motion For Protective Order Restricting Discovery:

The Motion is denied in part as follows: Traction shall, within 15 days, provide full and complete responses to: RFP 1-3, 8, 9, 16-18, 24-25, 27-28; and Srogs 4, 5, 12, 13-16, 20-21. Columbia Engineering Co. v. Joiner (1965) 231 Cal. App. 2d 837 cites numerous cases which support the general proposition that corporate actions may be challenged by a proceeding in equity. Traction cites several cases regarding the right of a director or former director to inspect corporate records. But the instant case is about a former shareholder's right to inspect. In its reply brief, Traction cites Wolf v. CDS DEVCO (2010) 185 Cal. App. 4th 903 at 919 in support of its argument. However, footnote 4 of that case states: "It must be emphasized that the subject ruling did not sustain the demurrers without leave to amend as to Wolf's shareholder petition with respect to Devco . . . "

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
